title: Host proof and closeout lane for issues 1859-1864

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Implement the core host proof/closeout lane for #1859, #1860, #1861, #1862, and the #1864 closeout posture umbrella.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not turn AW into a certification engine; keep host declarations repo-owned, compact, and selected through ordinary proof/implement paths.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Schema names, packet shape, and focused validation strategy when the ordinary proof path remains backwards compatible.
canonical_core.escalate_when: The implementation requires a new broad framework, breaks existing proof/report callers, or folds adjacent ignored/local-state issue #1863 into the high-assurance proof model.
canonical_core.next_action: Implement host-declared domain proof lanes, Verification host evidence concepts, architecture-principle proof pressure, and a compact proof decision packet.
proof_expectations: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_proof_cli.py -q -k "domain_proof_lane or proof_decision_packet or host_evidence_concept"
completion_criteria: Host repos can declare domain proof lanes without package code changes and proof --changed selects them with provenance, commands/manual evidence, review aids, evidence labels, and claim boundary.
Verification accepts declared host evidence concepts while distinguishing core, declared host, and invalid undeclared concepts.
A compact proof decision packet reports changed paths, lanes/obligations, commands/manual checks, assurance/Verification/architecture pressure, missing evidence, and safe claim state.
Architecture principle matches include path matcher reason, owner/authority, proof expectations or review aids, and claim boundary.
#1863 remains explicitly out of this core proof lane unless a small shared status hook becomes necessary.
